scope.summary:
Goal: implement a heading Outline / Table-of-Contents navigator panel for the native rich-text editor (E2), giving long documents the same wayfinding surface as the Obsidian Outline core plugin and Notion's table-of-contents block. This is the rich-text analog of the MT-006 code-editor outline; the two are distinct surfaces and must not be merged — MT-006 walks code symbols from tree-sitter, MT-056 walks the MT-011 block DocModel for Heading nodes. The panel walks the in-memory DocModel, collects all Heading blocks at levels 1 through 3, and renders an indented, clickable, collapsible tree. Clicking an entry scrolls the rich-text editor viewport to the target heading block and selects that block's range via the MT-012 caret/selection model. The tree live-updates on the next frame whenever headings are added, removed, retitled, or re-leveled.

The expected diff shape is: (a) one new Rust module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/outline_panel.rs (~300-450 lines) defining the OutlinePanel egui widget, the OutlineNode tree type, the build_outline() pure function over the DocModel, and the AccessKit registration; (b) a small wiring edit inside src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs so the widget exposes a scroll-to-block + select-block entry point the outline can call, and so the outline panel is mounted as a side tile next to the editor; (c) a kittest + unit test set proving outline construction, click-to-scroll-and-select, live update, and AccessKit node presence.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/outline_panel.rs
      * Define OutlineNode { block_id: String, level: u8, text: String, byte_offset: usize, children: Vec<OutlineNode>, collapsed: bool }.
      * Define pub fn build_outline(doc: &DocModel) -> Vec<OutlineNode>. This is a PURE function (no egui, no IO) so it is directly unit-testable. Iterate the DocModel block list in document order; for each block whose kind is Heading with level in 1..=3, record (block_id, level, plain_text(block), byte_offset_of_block). Then fold the flat ordered list into a nested tree by level: a heading of level N becomes a child of the most recent heading of level < N still on the stack (standard heading-stack algorithm). Headings that appear before any higher-level heading attach at the root. Byte offsets come from the DocModel block index (the same offset basis MT-012 uses for caret positioning) so the outline and the caret model agree on a single coordinate space.
      * Define pub struct OutlinePanel { roots: Vec<OutlineNode>, doc_revision: u64 } and pub fn OutlinePanel::show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, doc: &DocModel, editor: &mut RichEditorWidget). On show(): if doc.revision() != self.doc_revision, rebuild self.roots via build_outline(doc) and store the new revision (live update path — rebuild only when the DocModel revision counter advanced, so steady-state frames are cheap). Render the tree inside an egui::ScrollArea::vertical(); each node renders at horizontal indent = (level-1) * INDENT_PX with a collapse caret (egui::collapsing or a manual triangle toggle) for nodes that have children, followed by a selectable_label showing the heading text. On click of a node label, call editor.scroll_to_block(&node.block_id) and editor.select_block(&node.block_id).
      * AccessKit: the panel container registers author_id="rich-editor-outline", role=Tree. Each rendered entry registers author_id=format!("outline.heading.{}", node.block_id), role=TreeItem, with the heading text as the accessible name and an actions=[Press] so swarm agents and accessibility tooling can invoke scroll-to-heading deterministically. The Press action handler runs the same scroll_to_block + select_block path as a mouse click.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_outline.rs (or an inline #[cfg(test)] mod in outline_panel.rs if that is the crate's existing test convention) — unit + kittest coverage listed in proof_targets.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs
      * Add (or reuse if MT-012/MT-013 already exposed it) pub fn scroll_to_block(&mut self, block_id: &str) that sets the pending scroll target so the next layout pass brings that block into view (egui ScrollArea scroll_to_rect or a stored target offset resolved during render).
      * Add (or reuse) pub fn select_block(&mut self, block_id: &str) that delegates to the MT-012 caret/selection model to place an anchored selection across the target heading block's range — do NOT implement a second independent selection mechanism; route through the existing caret model so cross-pane selection (E5) stays consistent.
      * Mount OutlinePanel as a side tile (left or right) of the editor in whatever tile/dock surface the rich editor uses — reuse the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs): register the outline as its own pane so it is addressable by the swarm pane registry, not an ad-hoc child.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/mod.rs (or the module's existing mod-declaration file) — add `pub mod outline_panel;`.

Exact crates / egui / AccessKit patterns to use:
  - egui::ScrollArea::vertical().auto_shrink([false, false]) for the panel body.
  - egui::SelectableLabel / ui.selectable_label(false, text) for clickable entries; indent via ui.horizontal(|ui| { ui.add_space(indent_px); ... }).
  - Collapse toggle: store collapsed on OutlineNode and toggle on caret click; or use egui::CollapsingHeader for nodes with children. Keep collapse state across rebuilds keyed by block_id so a live update does not reset the user's expand/collapse choices (carry forward collapsed flags by block_id when rebuilding).
  - Scroll-to: prefer egui::Ui::scroll_to_rect / Response::scroll_to_me on the target block's response in the editor, recorded by the editor's render pass when it sees a pending scroll target matching the block_id.
  - AccessKit: use the project's existing AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) for author_id + role registration — the same pattern WP-011 and the other E2 MTs use (egui's accesskit integration via Response or the project's AccessKit id registry under accessibility/). Register role=Tree on the container and role=TreeItem per entry; attach an Action::Default / Press handler that fires the scroll+select.
  - Reuse the WP-011 shell primitives: register the outline as a pane in the pane registry (pane_registry.rs); route any command (e.g. a future "Go to heading" command) through the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) rather than a private dispatch; allocate AccessKit ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) so ids are unique and addressable.

Exact handshake_core / backend API bound: NONE. This MT is pure frontend. The outline is derived entirely from the in-memory MT-011 DocModel that already lives in the rich-text editor; there is no persistence, no document load, and no code-nav call. No PostgreSQL / EventLedger access in this MT (and there is no SQLite anywhere in the project — do not introduce any local store). If a needed DocModel accessor (block iteration order, block kind, heading level, plain-text extraction, per-block byte offset, or a document revision counter) is missing from the MT-011/MT-012/MT-013 surface, treat the gap as a TYPED BLOCKER: stop, name the exact missing accessor and the module that should own it, and escalate rather than reaching into DocModel internals or duplicating block-walk logic.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Confirm the DocModel (MT-011) exposes: an ordered block iterator, per-block kind discriminant with Heading level (1..=3 minimum; ignore deeper levels for this MT), a stable block_id per block, a plain-text extractor for a block's inline content, a per-block byte_offset, and a revision/dirty counter. If any is absent, raise a typed blocker (see above) before writing outline logic.
  2. Implement build_outline() as a pure function and test it in isolation first (no egui needed).
  3. Implement OutlinePanel::show with the revision-gated rebuild and the collapse-state carry-forward.
  4. Add scroll_to_block + select_block to rich_editor_widget.rs, routing selection through the MT-012 caret model.
  5. Register AccessKit Tree / TreeItem nodes and wire the Press action to the same scroll+select path as click.
  6. Mount the panel as a WP-011-registered pane (pane_registry.rs) beside the editor.
  7. Add the test module covering the four proof targets.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-011 (block DocModel) provides the heading blocks; MT-012 (caret/selection model) provides select_block and the shared coordinate/selection space; MT-013 provides the renderer surface that scroll_to_block drives. All three must be merged first. This MT blocks MT-044 (the parity + interconnection proof suite), which asserts heading-navigation parity against Obsidian/Notion.

Risk if missed: long rich-text documents have no heading-level navigation; a primary Obsidian/Notion wayfinding surface (the Outline core plugin / TOC block) is absent, and the MT-044 parity proof cannot pass.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: build_outline() over a DocModel with heading sequence h1 > h2 > h2 > h3 produces a nested tree where the h1 is a root, both h2 nodes are its children in document order, and the h3 is a child of the second h2 — with each node carrying the correct block_id and byte_offset from the DocModel — proven by a pure unit test.

AC-002: Only Heading blocks at levels 1-3 appear in the outline; non-heading blocks (paragraphs, lists, code blocks) and headings deeper than level 3 are excluded — proven by unit test over a mixed-block DocModel.

AC-003: Clicking an outline entry scrolls the rich-text editor so the target heading block is brought into view AND selects that block via the MT-012 caret model — proven by a kittest harness that inspects the editor's scroll target/offset and the caret selection range, and verified through the AccessKit event log.

AC-004: Adding a heading to the DocModel causes the outline tree to include the new heading on the next frame (revision-gated rebuild fires); the outline does not rebuild on frames where the DocModel revision is unchanged — proven by kittest.

AC-005: The AccessKit tree contains a container node author_id='rich-editor-outline' role=Tree and at least one entry node author_id='outline.heading.{block_id}' role=TreeItem with a Press action that triggers the same scroll+select path as a mouse click — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
AC-006: User expand/collapse state is preserved across a live rebuild (collapsing a node, then adding an unrelated heading, leaves the collapsed node collapsed) — proven by kittest.
AC-007: cargo test -p handshake-native passes with no failures across all outline tests; the outline reads only the in-memory DocModel and makes no backend, PostgreSQL, EventLedger, or SQLite call.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native build_outline_h1_h2_h2_h3 -- --nocapture => nested tree with correct parentage and byte offsets, all GREEN.

PT-002: kittest harness (cargo test -p handshake-native): click an outline entry, assert the editor scroll target resolves to the target block and the caret selection covers the target heading block (AccessKit event log captured).

PT-003: kittest harness (cargo test -p handshake-native): mutate the DocModel to add a heading, advance one frame, assert the outline tree now contains the new heading entry; assert no rebuild occurs on a frame with unchanged revision.

PT-004: AccessKit tree snapshot (cargo test -p handshake-native): assert node 'rich-editor-outline' role=Tree present and at least one 'outline.heading.{block_id}' role=TreeItem present with a Press action.
implementation_notes: build_outline() must be a pure function over &DocModel with no egui or IO so it can be unit-tested without a render harness.

Use a heading-stack fold to build nesting: maintain a stack of (level, &mut node); for each heading, pop while stack top level >= current level, then attach under the new top (or root if empty), then push.
Gate rebuilds on a DocModel revision/dirty counter (the same counter MT-012/MT-013 use for re-layout); never rebuild the tree on every frame.
Carry forward user collapse state by block_id across rebuilds so live updates do not reset the user's expand/collapse choices.

Route scroll-to via egui Response::scroll_to_me / Ui::scroll_to_rect recorded by the editor render pass; route block selection strictly through the MT-012 caret model — do not add a second selection mechanism.

Allocate AccessKit ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) and register the outline as a WP-011 pane-registry pane (pane_registry.rs) so it is swarm-addressable; do not invent ad-hoc ids.
Reuse the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) for any outline command dispatch rather than a private dispatch path.
This is the rich-text outline; keep it distinct from the MT-006 code-editor outline — different source (DocModel headings vs tree-sitter symbols), different module, different AccessKit ids.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT reuses WP-011 shell primitives (pane_registry.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, command_registry.rs, event_bus.rs); WP-011 must be merged before this MT can wire its pane/AccessKit/command registrations.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx — heading navigation behavior (how clicking a heading entry scrolls/focuses the target heading); port the scroll-to-heading interaction model to the native scroll_to_block + select_block path.
app/src/lib/editor/editor_commands.ts — heading block kinds and level enumeration; port the heading-kind/level vocabulary so the native build_outline() recognizes the same heading levels.
binds_backend_api: NONE — pure frontend; the outline is derived entirely from the in-memory MT-011 DocModel. No handshake_core call, no PostgreSQL, no EventLedger, no SQLite. Any missing DocModel accessor is a typed blocker, not a backend binding.

risks: RISK-001: Rebuilding the outline tree on every frame instead of only when the DocModel revision changes would cause large-document jank and waste — the live-update path must be revision-gated.

RISK-002: Duplicating block-walk or selection logic instead of reusing the MT-011 DocModel and MT-012 caret model would fragment the shared coordinate/selection space and break E5 cross-pane selection consistency.
RISK-003: Stale block_id after a heading is deleted — an outline entry may reference a block that no longer exists; clicking it would scroll/select a dangling block.
RISK-004: Resetting user expand/collapse state on every live rebuild would make the outline frustrating for long documents.

RISK-005: AccessKit id collisions between the rich-text outline ('outline.heading.{block_id}') and the MT-006 code outline, or duplicate ids if two rich editors are mounted, would make swarm/accessibility addressing ambiguous.
RISK-006: Reaching into DocModel internals when a needed accessor is missing (instead of raising a typed blocker) would couple the outline to private structure and break when MT-011 evolves.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Gate the rebuild strictly on the DocModel revision counter; add a kittest asserting no rebuild occurs on an unchanged-revision frame (AC-004 / PT-003).

MC-002: Route block selection only through the MT-012 caret model and scroll only through the editor's existing scroll path; prove via kittest that the caret selection and scroll target are driven by the shared model (AC-003).
MC-003: On click/Press, verify the block_id still exists in the live DocModel before scroll+select; if it is gone, skip silently and let the next revision-gated rebuild drop the stale entry.
MC-004: Carry forward collapse state by block_id across rebuilds; prove with a kittest (AC-006).

MC-005: Allocate AccessKit ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) with the 'outline.heading.{block_id}' namespace distinct from the MT-006 code-outline namespace; assert uniqueness in the AccessKit tree dump (AC-005).

MC-006: If any required DocModel accessor (block order, kind, heading level, plain-text, byte offset, revision counter) is absent, raise a typed blocker naming the exact missing accessor and owning module; do not access DocModel internals.
